title: Chat encoder silently encodes invalid team byte 0 when dest_team is None
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Line: 634

When encoding a chat message with dest_type=TEAM:
```python
if chat.dest_type == DestType.TEAM:
    parts.append(bytes([chat.dest_team or 0]))
```

If chat.dest_team is None, this encodes team byte as 0. However, 0 is NOT a valid Team value (the Team enum starts at 1 with BLUE=0x01).

This creates an invalid message that:
1. Will decode successfully but produce team_name='White' (fallback)
2. Violates the protocol specification
3. Masks a programming error (None team for TEAM dest_type)

Fix: Raise an error instead of silently using an invalid value:
```python
if chat.dest_type == DestType.TEAM:
    if chat.dest_team is None:
        raise ValueError('dest_team required when dest_type is TEAM')
    parts.append(bytes([chat.dest_team]))
```
notes:
Commit: f6c66a634
_encode_chat now raises ValueError if dest_type=TEAM and dest_team=None. Prevents invalid team byte 0. Updated _get_dest_uid and XML paths similarly if needed. P2 fixed.
